question.label: Cross-org alignment
question.name: Cross-org alignment — what about that work makes you hesitate?
question.description: Drills into the specific fear or constraint behind his hesitation in influence-heavy work.

answer: Part of it is confidence, I do not want to look unprepared in senior forums.

assessment.note: He names a confidence gap in senior forums as the reason he hesitates in cross-org alignment, a strong growth constraint; [THREAD-DEFERRED] confidence in senior forums should be picked up next session because drill cap requires stage progression.

new_queue.label: Direction
new_queue.name: When you imagine yourself eighteen months from now, what's actually different?

new_queue.label: Next-level definition
new_queue.name: What does senior scope mean to you in practice?

new_queue.label: Trust gap
new_queue.name: Where does stakeholder trust feel thinnest for you right now?

new_queue.label: Executor mode
new_queue.name: Where are you still operating more like an executor than a strategic owner?

new_queue.label: Next move
new_queue.name: What would your next move look like before we next meet?

issues: coverage: injected wellbeing into next question (0 touches after turn 4)

history.answer_excerpt: When conversations get political, I tend to retreat to what I can deliver directly.
I know I want bigger scope, but I still spend too much time in execution details.
I know I want bigger scope, but I still spend too much time in execution details.
When conversations get political, I tend to retreat to what I can deliver directly.
I can run projects, but shaping cross-org alignment is where I hesitate.
Part of it is confidence, I do not want to look unprepared in senior forums.
